hero.title: Gray Zone Warfare Wiki
hero.description: Explore maps, tasks, keys, vendors, weapons, ammo, loot routes, and patch changes for Gray Zone Warfare. Built for players who want faster progression, cleaner extractions, and better decision-making on Lamang Island.

gameFeature.title: Gray Zone Warfare Gameplay
gameFeature.description: Explore Lamang Island, complete tasks for vendors, find keys, gear up with the right weapons and ammo, and survive as a PMC operator in the jungle warfare extraction shooter.

gzwTaskGuide.title: Gray Zone Warfare Task Guide
gzwTaskGuide.intro: Tasks are the main progression engine in Gray Zone Warfare. This guide surfaces the early and midgame missions players search for most, with objectives, locations, rewards, and key unlocks.

steps.area: Pha Lang / Kiu Vongsa / Nam Thaven starter town
objectives: Check the shack on the outskirts
Check the area around the gas station
steps.why_it_matters: One of the first route-setting tasks that teaches players the starter town landmark loop.

objectives: Find and mark the cache in a shed at the town's lumberyard
steps.why_it_matters: Early weapon-progression task that builds Gunny reputation inside the safe starter loop.

objectives: Find the UNLRA medical supplies at the town's water tower
steps.why_it_matters: Lab Rat unlocks better sustain and med access for every future deployment.

steps.area: Pha Lang / Kiu Vongsa / Nam Thaven town hall
objectives: Mark the location of the body
steps.why_it_matters: Common bottleneck because players know the task name but forget the exact building and room.

objectives: Retrieve the intel from the restaurant attic
steps.why_it_matters: One of the earliest moments where players hit a key-gated progression wall.

objectives: Scout the main northern entry point to Ban Pa
Scout the main southern entry point to Ban Pa
steps.why_it_matters: Major progression break-point that opens Turncoat and pushes players out of the starter phase.

gzwInteractiveMap.title: Gray Zone Warfare Interactive Map
gzwInteractiveMap.intro: Gray Zone Warfare search traffic is heavily location-driven. Jump from a search term like Ban Pa, Fort Narith, or YBL-1 to the exact context you need: map grid, enemy type, tier, and run support.

items.label: Starter Towns
items.title: Know which base route you are actually on
content: Kiu Vongsa — starter town of Crimson Shield International — grid 141:164
Nam Thaven — starter town of Mithras Security Systems — grid 171:118
Pha Lang — starter town of Lamang Recovery Initiative — grid 202:162
quick_notes: These towns drive your early task flow
Locked rooms and task buildings differ but early progression structure is mirrored

items.label: YBL-1
items.title: The bunker that starts pushing players into mid-map routing
content: Type: Military underground bunker
Grid Position: 143:122
Frequently tied to recon and intel-style tasks
quick_notes: Acts as a bridge between starter progression and Ban Pa or Fort Narith routes

items.label: Ban Pa
items.title: Tier 2 village with cult-task and key pressure
content: Tier: 2
Locals Type: Rebels
LZ Callsign: INDIA
Grid Position: 205:137
quick_notes: Important for elder house, fishing hut, and quest-key searches
Common next step after players outgrow starter town farming

items.label: Fort Narith
items.title: The island's biggest military site
content: Tier: 3
Locals Type: LAF Soldiers
LZ Callsign: DELTA
Grid Position: 143:129
quick_notes: Combines tasks, keys, military loot, and danger in one zone
Many Fort Narith keys open rooms in or around the base and nearby Savanpha

items.label: Tiger Bay
items.title: Large city zone with dense objective and loot routing
content: Tier: 3
Locals Type: Rebels and LAF Soldiers
LZ Callsign: JULIET
Grid Position: 198:130
quick_notes: Strong search demand for mall, central armory, commander, and UNLRA task routes

items.label: Midnight Sapphire
items.title: High-value resort zone with late-game task traffic
content: Tier: 3
Locals Type: LAF Soldiers
LZ Callsign: HOTEL
Known for villa, waterfall, and hotel-based objectives
quick_notes: Often searched together with key-locked task routes

items.label: Blue Lagoon
items.title: Southern resort-side POI for exploration and route variety
content: Description: Small resort in the south near the mountains
Grid Position: 203:117
quick_notes: Useful as a recognizable anchor when planning southern rotations
Shows up in both task routing and code/padlock searches

gzwBeginnerGuide.title: Gray Zone Warfare Beginner Guide
gzwBeginnerGuide.intro: New players in Gray Zone Warfare usually struggle with mode choice, faction choice, sustain thresholds, helicopter usage, and gear-loss rules. These short cards turn first-session pain points into practical answers.

items.title: Pick the Right Mode First
items.summary: Warfare is the PvP mode. Joint Operations is the PvE mode.
details: Warfare: hostile players present across the island
Joint Operations: no hostile players, only AI enemies
Joint Operations allows safe progression and broad co-op learning
Warfare is built for higher-risk faction conflict and extraction pressure

items.title: Choose Your Faction by Geography
items.summary: Your faction changes your starting town, early routes, and travel time to high-value zones.
details: Crimson Shield International starts near Kiu Vongsa — slower early progression
Mithras Security Systems starts near Nam Thaven — sits between safer and hotter routes
Lamang Recovery Initiative starts near Pha Lang with fast access to contested zones
Approximate walk to Fort Narith: Lamang 5-10 min, Mithras 15-20 min, Crimson 25-30+ min

items.title: Protect What You Cannot Afford to Lose
items.summary: Most gear can be lost in deployment, but the Secure Lockbox protects its contents.
details: Items inside the Secure Lockbox stay protected on death
Melee weapons are not lost the same way as normal carried gear
Quest items sometimes survive in secure storage — prioritize quick hand-in routes

items.title: Watch Hydration and Energy Thresholds
items.summary: Low hydration and low energy directly hurt sustain and recovery.
details: Hydration below 50 percent: Thirsty — no blood regeneration
Hydration at 1 percent: Dehydrated — hazy vision
Energy below 50 percent: Hungry — no blood regeneration
Energy at 1 percent: Starving — hazy vision

items.title: Bleeding and Coma Kill Runs Faster Than Gunfights
items.summary: Beginner deaths often come from untreated injuries rather than headshots.
details: Light, medium, and severe bleeding all continue blood loss until treated
If blood drops below 50 percent, you can enter a coma
Destroyed lungs or liver can also cause coma
Squadmates have about 180 seconds to revive before death becomes permanent for that run

items.title: Use Helicopters as Protection and Misdirection
items.summary: The helicopter is not only transport — it is a survivability and positioning mechanic.
details: Players are immune to damage while onboard
After stepping off, there is a 4-second invulnerability window
Helicopter sound acts as a tactical distraction for AI and other players

items.title: Do Not AFK Outside Safe Areas
items.summary: The AFK timer can cost gear if it removes you from a live deployment.
details: The inactivity limit is 15 minutes
If removed while outside the FOB, normal leave-deployment loss rules apply

gzwKeysGuide.title: Gray Zone Warfare Keys Guide
gzwKeysGuide.intro: Key-gated progression is one of the biggest friction points in Gray Zone Warfare. Find the right key before you waste another run — where it drops, what it unlocks, and which tasks it unblocks.

items.where_to_find: In pockets of enemies in Kiu Vongsa
In pockets of enemies in Pha Lang
items.unlocks: Attic storage in the Pha Lang restaurant

items.where_to_find: In pockets of enemies in Nam Thaven
items.unlocks: Attic storage in the Nam Thaven restaurant

items.where_to_find: In pockets of enemies in Sawmill
items.unlocks: Office building at the Tonmai Hainy Lumber Company
items.behind_lock: 1x Firearms Box; 1x First Aid Cabinet; loose weapons

items.key_name: Pha Lang Airfield Storage Office Key
items.where_to_find: In pockets of enemies in Pha Lang Airfield
items.unlocks: Office room in the storage building at the airfield

items.where_to_find: In pocket of the Ban Pa leader; can also spawn in Ban Pa
items.unlocks: Second-floor room inside the Elder's House

items.where_to_find: In pockets of enemies in Ban Pa
items.unlocks: Fishing hut next to the Elder's House at 205:137
items.related_task: Ban Pa loot and side-route search traffic
items.behind_lock: 1x Firearms box; 1x Equipment Box; 1x Ammo Box

items.key_name: Fort Narith Outskirts Improvised Armory Key
items.unlocks: Grey two-story house back room in Savanpha Village at 146:128

gzwMoneyGuide.title: Gray Zone Warfare Money Guide
gzwMoneyGuide.subtitle: Recover your cash flow with repeatable starter tasks, better loot priorities, and cleaner sell decisions. This module focuses on routes and actions that create money without forcing high-risk fights.
gzwMoneyGuide.intro: Money in Gray Zone Warfare comes from two reliable sources: task rewards and disciplined looting. Early tasks already provide cash, reputation, and useful items, while weapon containers and valuables can be sold through the FOB vendor network. A good recovery loop is simple: run a nearby task, loot efficiently, sell intelligently, and use the next payout to restock instead of overbuilding a kit.

items.title: Start with nearby paid tasks
items.summary: Use early and mid-route tasks as the base of your economy loop before taking larger gear risks.
data_points: Meds Wanted: 400 EXP, +100 Lab Rat reputation, $1,200, 2x Combat Tourniquet
Tools of the Trade: 150 EXP, +105 Artisan reputation, $400
What's in Store: 2,000 EXP, +200 Gunny reputation, $3,300, 1x HARDE Case

items.title: Prioritize firearm containers when looting
items.summary: Weapon containers are high-value stops because they can hold guns, magazines, and weapon parts.
data_points: CSI Firearms Box: 8x6 loot container for weapons and weapon parts
UN Firearms Box: 7x4 loot container for weapons and weapon parts
Firearms Box: 8x3 loot container for weapons and weapon parts
Military Firearms Crate can include AK308, AK74, and SVD Dragunov magazines

items.title: Sell valuables to the best-paying vendor
items.summary: Valuable loot can be sold to any vendor, but different buyers pay more for different items.
data_points: Use the listed best buyer when you want maximum return instead of convenience
Valuables can be sold to any vendor for 50% less than the highest shown price
Prioritize what to take back to the FOB and what to leave behind

items.title: Turn cash into better vendor access
items.summary: Your economy gets stronger when task money also pushes vendor reputation and unlocks better stock.
data_points: Tasks are the fastest way to gain EXP and often reward money, items, and vendor reputation
Some tasks unlock new vendor stock
Update 0.4 rebalanced rewards to place greater emphasis on money rather than items
Completing a task for the first time grants a larger reputation boost

gzwWeaponsGuide.title: Gray Zone Warfare Weapons Guide
gzwWeaponsGuide.subtitle: Track the core rifle platforms, their calibers, and where each family fits into the broader weapon pool. The goal is to help players quickly understand platform differences before investing in magazines, ammo, and attachments.
gzwWeaponsGuide.intro: Gray Zone Warfare's weapon ecosystem is built around caliber families, compatible magazines, and attachment ecosystems. The official weapon pages and vendor pages show which platforms exist in the game, while the latest Spearhead devlog confirms that weight, sway, ADS behavior, reload handling, and ballistics all matter more in the current direction of the game. That means weapon choice is not just about raw firepower anymore.

items.name: AK-12
notes: Shares caliber family with AK-74M, AK-74N, and AKS-74U
Good platform for players running the 5.45 ammo family

items.name: AK-15
notes: Uses the same caliber family as AKM, AKMN, AKMSN, and SKS
Versatile mid-range platform with broad ammo availability

items.name: AK-19
notes: Shares caliber family with M4A1, DDM4, and CQ A1
Bridges the AK-pattern platform with NATO-caliber ammo access

items.name: AK-74M
notes: Widely available early and a solid baseline for the 5.45 caliber tree

items.name: SIG MCX
notes: Dedicated platform caliber, best treated as a standalone build
Niche but powerful option for players comfortable with a single-caliber loadout

items.name: 0.4 Handling Changes
notes: Update 0.4 adds weapon weight effects on ergonomics, sway, acceleration, and ADS
Weapon stats revamped to emphasize handling and reload behavior
Ballistics calculations improved to make weapons and ammunition more viable

gzwAmmoGuide.title: Gray Zone Warfare Ammo Guide
gzwAmmoGuide.subtitle: Use this caliber overview to connect ammo families to the weapons that fire them. It gives players a practical map for inventory planning, magazine selection, and cross-platform ammo sharing.
gzwAmmoGuide.intro: Ammo choice in Gray Zone Warfare is tied directly to weapon platform and magazine compatibility. The game wiki's ammunition overview shows which calibers are used by which rifles, DMRs, and bolt-action weapons, while the latest devlog confirms that ballistics and overall ammo viability are being reworked in Spearhead. That makes caliber awareness one of the easiest ways to avoid bad loadout mistakes.

items.planning_note: Best treated as a dedicated platform caliber rather than a shared early inventory staple.
Useful when you want multiple AK-pattern rifles to draw from the same caliber family.
A broad rifle caliber family that spans both western-style rifles and the AK-19 platform.
Supports both assault-rifle and SKS setups, useful for players switching between roles.
Shared across heavier precision-oriented weapon choices instead of lighter assault-rifle families.
Spearhead update notes confirm ballistics calculations are being improved and weapons plus ammunition are rebalanced for better viability.

gzwVendorsGuide.title: Gray Zone Warfare Vendors Guide
gzwVendorsGuide.subtitle: See what each vendor specializes in, which tasks unlock extra access, and how vendor reputation gates better stock. This makes progression planning much easier when you want a specific type of gear instead of random upgrades.
gzwVendorsGuide.intro: Vendors are the backbone of Gray Zone Warfare progression. They sell gear, buy looted items, and unlock better stock as your reputation climbs. The vendor pages also show that several traders are linked by task chains, so smart progression means not only earning reputation, but doing it with the vendor that opens the category you want next.

items.specialty: Helmets, gear, body armor, containers, tools
items.progression_note: Good for players who want early progression tied to protection, storage, and general field gear.
Primary medical and sustain vendor for healing items, supplies, and utility storage.

items.specialty: Weapons, magazines, ammo, throwables, sights, weapon attachments
items.progression_note: Best early anchor when your goal is to improve rifle choice, attachments, and ammunition access.

items.specialty: Weapons, magazines, ammo, sights, attachments, helmets, containers
items.unlock_path: Requires completing Native Negotiations from Lab Rat
items.progression_note: Strong vendor to prioritize once you want more technical control over attachments and weapon builds.

items.specialty: Weapons, magazines, ammo, sights, attachments, gear
items.unlock_path: Requires completing Invaders From Afar from Artisan
items.progression_note: Fits players who want another weapon-focused branch while expanding wearable gear options.

items.specialty: Weapons, ammo, sights, attachments, helmets, gear, body armor, containers
items.unlock_path: Requires completing The Shadow Over Ban Pa from Handshake
items.progression_note: One of the broadest inventories — combining weapon support with armor and container progression.

gzwHealthSystemGuide.title: Gray Zone Warfare Health System Guide
gzwHealthSystemGuide.subtitle: Gray Zone Warfare uses a body-part and vital-pool system instead of a simple HP bar. The fastest way to survive is to identify the root problem and use the correct treatment item immediately.
gzwHealthSystemGuide.intro: In Gray Zone Warfare, wounds, bruises, organ damage, blood loss, pain, and hydration all interact with each other. A player can look alive for a moment and still collapse if bleeding, organ damage, or dehydration is not handled in the right order. This guide is built for quick mid-raid reference so players can match a symptom to the right fix.

content: Tracks body parts plus vital pools such as blood and hydration — no traditional HP model
Survival depends on treating the cause of a problem, not only the visible symptom
Blood can regenerate naturally, but recovery stops when hydration or energy drops too low
Wounds are marked in red and happen when a projectile bypasses protective gear
Light wounds can heal over time; medium and severe wounds need treatment
Bandages treat wounds; tourniquets stop bleeding but do not heal the wound
Bruises are marked in purple and happen when armor stops a hit but impact still causes trauma
Bruises create pain but usually heal naturally over time
Pain and suffering can lead to tremors, which make weapon handling worse
Arm and leg bones can be damaged by impacts or falls
Leg bone destruction causes limping, removes sprint and jump, and slows stamina recovery
Flexible Splints are the standard treatment for broken or fractured limbs
Brain, heart, lungs, and liver can all be damaged
Destroyed vital organs such as the heart or brain result in immediate death
Surgery kits are used to restore damaged or destroyed organs
Blood loss causes worsening symptoms and can end in coma
Dizzy status appears until blood is brought above 5,000 ml or hydration and energy are restored
Blood bags are used to replenish lost blood
Thirsty starts when hydration drops below 50 percent and stops blood regeneration
Dehydrated causes hazy vision and continues until hydration is brought above 1 percent
Hungry starts when energy drops below 50 percent and also stops blood regeneration
ARF Trauma Bandage 4 — heals wounds, 4 charges
Civilian Gauze — heals wounds, 3 charges
Flexible Splint — repairs fractures, 5 charges
Basic Surgical & Suture Kit — treats organ injury, 6 charges
Large Blood Bag — 500 ml per charge, 2 charges
Meloxicam and Ibalin — painkillers to reduce pain effects
First: stop active bleeding
Then: fix wounds and fractures
Then: stabilize organs when needed
Then: restore blood volume if symptoms continue
Finally: top up hydration and energy so regeneration can resume
To heal a teammate, medical items must be in Tactical Rig, Tactical Belt, or Pockets
Examine the injured player first to see what needs intervention
Medical bags save inventory space but items must be pulled out before use

gzwFactionsGuide.title: Gray Zone Warfare Factions Guide
gzwFactionsGuide.subtitle: Gray Zone Warfare has three playable PMCs with different lore, base camp positions, starter towns, and task routing. Your faction choice also decides whether you can squad up with specific friends.
gzwFactionsGuide.intro: Faction choice is not just cosmetic. It affects your starting side of the map, your accessible early routes, and parts of your task flow. The most important rule for groups: everyone must be in the same faction.

items.name: Lamang Recovery Initiative
items.summary: A billionaire-funded PMC that presents itself as a rescue and recovery force around Ground Zero.
highlights: Public image centers on survivor search, rescue, and clean-up
Uses small, highly professional security details to protect teams
Starter town: Pha Lang — fast access to contested zones

items.name: Mithras Security Systems
items.summary: A soldier-first PMC that values field experience and military competence.
highlights: Markets itself as a company run by soldiers for soldiers
Accepts experienced operators and emphasizes merit and spoils-sharing culture
Starter town: Nam Thaven — between safer and hotter routes

items.name: Crimson Shield International
items.summary: A high-success PMC known for ruthless effectiveness and a harsher reputation.
highlights: Known in the lore for a 95 percent success rate
Attracts action-focused operators but has a reputation for collateral damage
Starter town: Kiu Vongsa — commonly treated as friendlier for slower early progression

items.name: What Actually Changes
items.summary: Faction choice affects more than flavor text.
highlights: Your faction decides your starting area on Lamang Island
Some available tasks differ by faction
Friends must join the same faction to play together
The player can wipe the character every 7 days if a reset is needed

gzwArmorGuide.title: Gray Zone Warfare Armor Guide
gzwArmorGuide.subtitle: Armor effectiveness depends on NIJ class, where the plates actually cover your body, and what material the armor is made from. Paying more does not automatically mean better protection in every situation.
gzwArmorGuide.intro: The game uses NIJ-style armor classes and a location-based plate system. When you inspect an armor piece, the important question is not only the class number but also whether the plate protects the front and back areas you are likely to expose. Material matters too, because different armor types resist rounds differently even when durability changes.
notes: Gray Zone Warfare uses the NIJ system — protection levels from worst to best: I, IIA, IIA+, IIIA, IIIA+, III, III+, III++
The visible plate locations on inspect show where penetration protection exists
Armor material matters: UHMWPE is low resistance, Ceramic is medium, Steel is higher resistance
Plate carriers combine storage space with armor; plain vests pair with an unarmored tactical rig
Update 0.4 UI now shows armor levels more clearly and ammo details display the lowest armor class a round cannot penetrate

gzwBestLoadouts.title: Gray Zone Warfare Best Loadouts
gzwBestLoadouts.subtitle: The most useful Gray Zone Warfare loadouts are not flashy theorycraft builds. They are kits built around survival, extraction, task efficiency, armor coverage, medical readiness, and the right amount of mobility.
gzwBestLoadouts.intro: This module focuses on real deployment archetypes players actually use: a cheap beginner rifle setup, a quiet solo PvE kit, a heavier squad combat build, and an objective-first task run setup. Each one is designed around proven game systems such as stamina, hydration, suppression, armor coverage, and medical preparedness.

items.name: Beginner Budget Run
items.summary: A low-risk setup for starter town tasks and early cash recovery.
highlights: Affordable rifle such as the AKM — commonly recommended as a cheap starting platform
Armor goal: front-and-back protection without overloading weight
Medical core: bandage, splint, blood bag, painkiller
Playstyle: avoid long fights, finish the objective, and extract

items.name: Solo PvE Stealth Kit
items.summary: Built for moving quietly, finishing tasks, and surviving alone.
highlights: Suppressed weapons are strongly favored for solo play
Use lighter armor and controlled movement to manage stamina and hydration
Prioritize mission items and key loot over greed
Scout compounds before entry and treat exfil as the real win

items.name: Squad PvEvP Heavy Kit
items.summary: A stronger group build for contested zones and harder fights.
highlights: Plate carriers with front-and-back coverage and stronger NIJ classes when possible
Medical items in accessible slots so teammates can treat each other fast
Move with multiple angles, shared supplies, and revive support
For late-game areas, suppressed fire and at least one teammate are strongly recommended

items.name: Task-Focused Utility Loadout
items.summary: A balanced kit for missions where travel, searching, and extraction matter more than kill count.
highlights: Carry only what supports the task: weapon, meds, hydration, and room for quest items
Faction choice changes route efficiency — each faction starts in a different town
Task flow was reworked again in Update 0.4
The best task loadout keeps weight controlled and leaves room to bring objective items home

gzwPveVsPvpGuide.title: Gray Zone Warfare PvE vs PvP Guide
gzwPveVsPvpGuide.intro: Gray Zone Warfare supports both Warfare and Joint Operations. Progression is shared between modes, so many players use Joint Operations to learn locations and tasks before taking the same character into Warfare.

items.warfare: Full PvPvE — rival factions can fight each other across the map
items.joint_operations: PvE only — players cannot attack or loot each other, only AI enemies

items.warfare: Best for players who want unpredictable encounters and faction pressure from the start
items.joint_operations: Best for beginners who want to learn extraction flow, healing, map routes, and task objectives

items.warfare: Playable solo, but helicopter timing and enemy players make mistakes more punishing
items.joint_operations: More forgiving for solo learning because enemy pressure comes from AI rather than human ambushes

items.warfare: Solo or squad play, up to four players
items.joint_operations: Solo or squad play, up to four players

items.warfare: Enemy PMCs can shoot you anywhere on the map outside brief helicopter arrival protection
items.joint_operations: No hostile player damage or player looting between participants

items.warfare: Factions fight over COP control; rival players become part of the outpost battle flow
items.joint_operations: First faction captures the COP by clearing AI; after that, all factions can use it as a shared safe zone

items.warfare: Go here for extraction tension, faction conflict, and dynamic encounters around routes and outposts
items.joint_operations: Go here to finish tasks, learn layouts, test kits, or farm experience with fewer variables
Shared character progression — progress made here also carries into Warfare

items.warfare: Extraction veterans, confident duos and squads, and players who enjoy contested objectives
items.joint_operations: New players, task-focused players, and anyone practicing routes, healing flow, or vendor progression

gzwLocationsGuide.title: Gray Zone Warfare Locations Guide
gzwLocationsGuide.intro: Gray Zone Warfare search intent is heavily location-driven because players are constantly moving between starter towns, task objectives, loot areas, and military compounds. The major POIs below cover the most useful homepage anchors.

entries.name: Kiu Vongsa
entries.role: Starter town for Crimson Shield International
entries.why: Common early-game destination for CSI routes and nearby farm-side movement

entries.name: Nam Thaven
entries.role: Starter town for Mithras Security Systems
entries.why: Core opening area for Mithras players and early route planning anchor

entries.name: Pha Lang
entries.role: Starter town for Lamang Recovery Initiative
entries.why: Main early-game home area for LRI progression and southern movement

entries.name: Sawmill
entries.role: Tier 2 zone with an old sawmill
entries.why: Recognizable step up from starter areas and frequent mid-game target

entries.name: Hunter's Paradise
entries.why: Players search this for tasks, route planning, and nearby points of interest

entries.name: Ban Pa
entries.role: Small town on the east side near water
entries.why: Useful for task routing and eastern travel planning

entries.name: Pha Lang Airfield
entries.why: Important for navigation awareness and transport-adjacent geography

entries.name: YBL-1
entries.why: Standout military site with a very different feel from open settlements

entries.name: Fort Narith
entries.role: Biggest military site and second biggest airfield on the island
entries.why: Most searched high-threat location due to scale, military value, and recurring task interest

entries.name: Tiger Bay
entries.role: Biggest city with a large commercial center
entries.why: Major urban landmark and one of the clearest late-route city destinations

entries.name: Blue Lagoon
entries.role: Small resort in the south near the mountains
entries.why: Named landmark players recognize quickly when planning southern routes

entries.name: Major COPs
entries.role: Strategic respawn, restock, and control points
entries.why: Captured COPs can serve as respawn points with access to lockers and vendors

entries.name: Shared PvE COP Access
entries.why: In PvE, once one faction secures a COP from AI, all factions can use it as a shared safe zone

gzwEditionDifferences.title: Gray Zone Warfare Edition Differences
gzwEditionDifferences.intro: Edition shopping in Gray Zone Warfare is mostly about storage, container space, and early inventory flexibility. Standard starts with the highest listed base cash but the smallest locker.

items.standard: Basic healing kit, armor, mags, and extra rifles
items.tactical: Grenades, foregrip, flashlight, headset, biocular night vision
items.elite: Grenades, suppressors, tactical device, monocular night vision, stronger med stack
items.supporter: Grenades, rifle suppressor, tactical device, scopes, top storage advantage

items.tactical: More storage and gear upgrade path
items.supporter: GZW T-shirt, gloves, supporter color name, exclusive badge, all DLC included

items.standard: Players who want the lowest entry price and do not mind tighter stash management
items.tactical: Players who value more locker room and a practical upgrade over standard
items.elite: Players who want a larger lockbox and a more advanced combat starter kit
items.supporter: Players who want the biggest storage, widest bonus package, and supporter cosmetics

gzwUpdateRoadmap.title: Gray Zone Warfare Update Roadmap
gzwUpdateRoadmap.intro: The current Gray Zone Warfare update story is centered on the 0.4 Spearhead release cycle. The roadmap established long-term beats while recent news posts turned 0.4 into a much larger milestone.

items.summary: The roadmap was reshaped to focus more on nearer features while keeping long-term expansion plans visible.
highlights: Winds of War positioned for Spring 2025
Dark Revelations positioned for Fall 2025
2026+ roadmap named Battle Forge, Shadow Strike, Rising Tensions, and Ground Zero
Major updates planned roughly every six months

items.summary: Update 0.4 was formally reintroduced as Spearhead — a much broader milestone than its earlier working title.
highlights: Update renamed from Dark Revelations to Spearhead
Release window set for March 2026
Three devlogs announced leading into launch
Supporter Edition owners promised early access to the preview build

items.summary: The second Spearhead devlog focused on map, enemy, weapon, and world expansion.
highlights: 25+ new locations added in varying sizes
New micro-biomes: swamps, bamboo forests, and burned forests
Existing locations rebuilt and polished
AI enemies redesigned into seven distinct factions

items.summary: The third Spearhead devlog revealed major gameplay-loop and progression overhauls.
highlights: Spearhead launches on March 31, 2026
Supporter Edition early access on March 27, 2026
New onboarding area and skippable tutorial added
In-game Field Manual with more than 110 pages added
Total tasks now exceed 250 including 100+ new tasks and contracts

items.summary: The official news feed already points to follow-up work beyond the core launch.
highlights: NVIDIA DLSS 4.5 planned for patches following Spearhead
Long-term roadmap still points toward Battle Forge, Shadow Strike, Rising Tensions, and Ground Zero
Developers continue to position Gray Zone Warfare as a growing tactical sandbox

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Gray Zone Warfare

questions.question: What is Gray Zone Warfare?
questions.answer: Gray Zone Warfare is a PvE-first open-world tactical extraction shooter developed by MADFINGER Games. It launched on Steam Early Access on April 30, 2024 and takes place on Lamang Island where players operate as PMC contractors.

questions.question: Does Gray Zone Warfare have a PvP mode?
questions.answer: Yes. Gray Zone Warfare offers two modes: Warfare (full PvPvE where rival factions can engage each other) and Joint Operations (PvE-only with no hostile player encounters). Character progression is shared between both modes.

questions.question: What factions are in Gray Zone Warfare?
questions.answer: Gray Zone Warfare has three playable PMC factions: Crimson Shield International (starter town Kiu Vongsa), Mithras Security Systems (starter town Nam Thaven), and Lamang Recovery Initiative (starter town Pha Lang). Players in the same squad must choose the same faction.

questions.question: How do tasks work in Gray Zone Warfare?
questions.answer: Tasks are missions given by vendors like Handshake, Gunny, and Lab Rat. Completing tasks earns EXP, cash, items, and vendor reputation. Higher reputation unlocks better vendor stock. Update 0.4 Spearhead added over 100 new tasks and contracts for a total exceeding 250.

questions.question: What is the Gray Zone Warfare Update 0.4 Spearhead?
questions.answer: Spearhead is the major 0.4 update launching on March 31, 2026. It adds 25+ new locations, seven redesigned AI factions, 100+ new tasks, a full system rework for weapons and ballistics, a new onboarding area, and an in-game Field Manual with over 110 pages.

questions.question: How many editions does Gray Zone Warfare have?
questions.answer: Gray Zone Warfare is available in four editions: Standard (10x25 locker, 2x2 lockbox), Tactical (10x35 locker, SMG package), Elite (10x45 locker, DMR package, 3x2 lockbox), and Supporter (10x60 locker, 3x3 lockbox, all DLC, exclusive cosmetics).
